THE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM IS ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS ISSUED BY DEFENDANTS, FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER, AND FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS)ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ANA ZELEDON'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE), SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE), REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (SET ONE), AND REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, (SET ONE) AND FOR SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. THE REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION OF THE JUDGE PRO TEM IS ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANTS' RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE), SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE), AND REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS (SET ONE) AND FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS SHALL PROVIDE CODE-COMPLIANT, VERIFIED RESPONSES TO ALL REQUESTS IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT WITHIN TWO WEEKS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. IF DEFENDANTS FAIL TO PROVIDE CODE-COMPLIANT, VERIFIED RESPONSES WITHIN TWO WEEKS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER, SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000 SHALL BE PAID BY DEFENDANTS AND THEIR COUNSEL TO PLAINTIFF. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas Issued By Defendants, For An In Camera Review Of Plaintiffs Private Records, For A Protective Order, And For Sanctions Against Defendants Counsel
Off Calendar Feb-11-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kristen Vierhaus, Esq. 650-513-6111 of Thompson Law Offices for Plaintiff.
Homaira Khalji, Esq. 650-989-5400 of Clapp Mornory Vucinich Beeman Scheley for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues motion to quash, argues sanctions. Plaintiff argues and requests to re-examine sanctions. The Court declines to increase sanctions on the second motion. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANA ZELEDON'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas Issued By Defendants, For An In Camera Review Of Plaintiffs Private Records, For A Protective Order, And For Sanctions Against Defendants' Counsel.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to quash subpoenas issued by defendants, for a protective order, and for sanctions against defendants' counsel is granted. Plaintiff's objections to the scope of each of the 12 subject subpoenas are sustained for overbreadth and privacy. As to the subpoenas served on plaintiff's medical providers, a personal injury plaintiff's waiver "extends only to information relating to the medical conditions in question, and does not automatically open all of a plaintiff's past medical history to scrutiny." (Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844, 849.) The scope of the inquiry permitted depends upon the nature of the injuries which the patient-litigant himself has brought before the court. (Id. at 864; see also Lantz v. Superior Court (1994) 28 Cal.App.4th 1839, 1853-1857 [court is required to balance patient's interest in her medical records against the stated need for the requested records].) Here, just as in Hallendorf v. Superior Court (1978) 85 Cal.App.3d 553, the discovery sought by defendants, which would require plaintiff's physicians to disclose all treatment rendered to her since 1995, without limitation as to the medical conditions which she has put at issue, is "clearly overbroad." (Id. at 557.) The subpoenas to plaintiff's employers for all employment records are likewise overbroad and violated plaintiff's right to privacy.

Every page of documents produced in response to any and all of those 12 subpoenas must be turned over to plaintiff's counsel within five days, accompanied by a declaration of defense counsel stating under oath that all documents received from each and every one of the 12 challenged subpoenas has been turned over to plaintiff's counsel, and averring that no copies of any of those documents have been copied or saved by defendant or any of their attorneys or other agents. Defendants may serve new, appropriately tailored, subpoenas if they wish, subject to all applicable provisions of the Code, regarding the specific injuries and damages claimed here by plaintiff. Sanctions in the amount of $4,950.00 are awarded to plaintiff against defense counsel.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Response To Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One), Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One), And Requests For Admission, (Set One) And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-11-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kristen Vierhaus, Esq. 650-513-6111 of Thompson Law Offices for Plaintiff.
Homaira Khalji, Esq. 650-989-5400 of Clapp Mornory Vucinich Beeman Scheley for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues motion to quash, argues sanctions. Plaintiff argues and requests to re-examine sanctions. The Court declines to increase sanctions on the second motion. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANA ZELEDON'S Motion To Compel Defendants Response To Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One), Requests For Production Of Documents (Set One), And Requests For Admission, (Set One) And For Sanctions.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to compel defendants' responses to form interrogatories (set one), special interrogatories (set one), and requests for admissions (set one) and for sanctions is granted. Defendants shall provide code-compliant, verified responses to all requests in the separate statement within two weeks of notice of this order. If defendants fail to provide code-compliant, verified responses within two weeks of notice of this order, sanctions in the amount of $1,000 shall be paid by defendants and their counsel to plaintiff.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
